summary: Completed comprehensive testing of The Diia Thesis PDF library application. All backend APIs working correctly, frontend UI fully functional with proper navigation, PDF reader, themes section, and dark mode toggle.
passed_tests: Backend /api/health endpoint returns 200 with correct status
Backend /api/book endpoint returns complete metadata with 8 themes
Navigation bar renders with Home, Read the Thesis, About links and Download button
Dark mode toggle switches between light and dark themes correctly
Hero section displays title, subtitle, description, and theme pills
Metadata card shows Document Type, Subject, Geographic Focus, Language, Access, Published, Comparative Cases
Read the Thesis CTA button scrolls to PDF reader section
Download PDF button links to PDF file
PDF reader section loads with 'Read the Thesis' heading
PDF toolbar present with prev/next page buttons, page indicator, zoom controls, fit width, download, open-in-tab buttons
PDF renders and shows page 1 of 144 pages correctly
Page navigation works (next page, prev page buttons functional)
Zoom in/out and fit width controls work properly

Themes section shows all 8 theme cards: Civic AI & Digital Government, Hromada, Human Dignity & Participation, War-Time Resilience, Honest AI & Transparency, Linguistic Sovereignty, Democracy vs. Surveillance, Institutional Design
Philosophical framework section displays Marx, Wojtyla, Cossack Tradition, Catholic Social Teaching
Footer renders with citation, download link, and external links
Active nav link indicator works (highlighted link changes as user scrolls - font weight 400 to 600)
Smooth scrolling navigation between sections functional
